questions.q: A point charge of 5 μC is placed at a distance of 20 cm from another identical charge. Calculate the force of repulsion between the two charges. (k = 9 × 10^9 N m^2 C^-2)
Fill in the blank: The force between two charges is _______________ to the product of the charges and _______________ to the square of the distance between them.
State the principle of superposition of electric charges.
questions.answer: The principle of superposition states that the total electric field at a point due to multiple charges is the vector sum of the electric fields due to each individual charge.

questions.q: Which of the following statements is true? (A) Electric charge is conserved in an inelastic collision. (B) Electric charge is not conserved in an elastic collision. (C) Electric charge is conserved in all types of collisions. (D) Electric charge is not conserved in any type of collision.
A sphere of radius 10 cm has a charge of 20 μC distributed uniformly over its surface. Calculate the electric field at a point 15 cm from the center of the sphere.
What is the SI unit of electric charge?
Two charges, each of magnitude 2 μC, are placed 10 cm apart. Calculate the force of repulsion between them. (k = 9 × 10^9 N m^2 C^-2)
State True or False: The force between two charges is independent of the medium in which they are placed.
A point charge of 5 μC is placed at the center of a spherical shell of radius 10 cm. Calculate the electric flux through the shell.
Fill in the blank: The electric field at a point due to a point charge is _______________ to the distance from the charge.
